> @mikeharder I am more curious why PR #35105 can be merged, should tsv fail when service team adding new manual authored swaggers after it converted to TypeSpec?

There is a feature request for this, but not yet implemented:

- https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/issues/34406

I will add you to the mail thread with more context.

events.description: > I believe this PR is actually doing two things: >  > 1. Reverting #35105, which was merged un-in

details.body:
> I believe this PR is actually doing two things:
> 
> 1. Reverting #35105, which was merged un-intentionally
> 2. Adding typespec-generated swagger for 2025-06-01
> 
> Instead of combining these in a single PR, I think the cleanest path forward would be:
> 
> * Run `git revert 35105`, to directly undo the mistake.  This revert PR may have breaking changes, but we are just reverting your spec to the last known good status, so it should be safer to bypass failing checks.
> * Create a new

events.description: Agree but this design has already been approved and shipped.  FYI,  this PR this is actually the sec

details.body: Agree but this design has already been approved and shipped.  FYI,  this PR this is actually the second release of this API as the first one had to be reverted as it was a handcrafted swagger and not one generated by TypeSpec, the first one being https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/35105, and in that PR I believe this very point of Enums vs Booleans was discussed and the call was to go with boolean.

events.description: Arm reviewers, note that I added one more change to this PR, I removed a property, `storageAccountTy

details.body: Arm reviewers, note that I added one more change to this PR, I removed a property, `storageAccountType` on the `DataDisk` model that we discovered would cause an runtime error if left in.  The issue is that the `storageAccountType` property was defined in the `ManagedDisk` model which was added to the `DataDisk` model thus duplicating it.  This duplication would cause a runtime error if `storageAccountType` was set in both the `DataDisk` and `ManagedDisk` models.

events.description: The new synchronous paging path bypasses the client's default/merged `Context`. `listSinglePage()` a

details.body: The new synchronous paging path bypasses the client's default/merged `Context`. `listSinglePage()` and `listNextSinglePage()` hardcode `Context.NONE`, and the overloads that accept a `Context` don't merge with any client-level context. This can drop critical per-request metadata (e.g., tracing values, policy hints, headers from `AddHeadersFromContextPolicy`) compared to the async path which merges/propagates context. Fix by merging the provided context with the client’s context (or using `this.c

events.description: The authentication snippet uses `AzureCloud.AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD` with `AzureProfile`, but the SDK’s m

details.body: The authentication snippet uses `AzureCloud.AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD` with `AzureProfile`, but the SDK’s management-plane samples typically use `com.azure.core.management.AzureEnvironment` (e.g., `AzureEnvironment.AZURE`) with `AzureProfile`. Please update the README sample to use the correct cloud/environment type for `AzureProfile` in this library, and align the explanatory text accordingly to avoid confusing users with a non-compiling example.

events.description: The authentication snippet uses `AzureCloud.AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD` with `AzureProfile`, but the SDK’s m

details.body: The authentication snippet uses `AzureCloud.AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD` with `AzureProfile`, but the SDK’s management-plane samples typically use `com.azure.core.management.AzureEnvironment` (e.g., `AzureEnvironment.AZURE`) with `AzureProfile`. Please update the README sample to use the correct cloud/environment type for `AzureProfile` in this library, and align the explanatory text accordingly to avoid confusing users with a non-compiling example.

events.description: The constructor no longer initializes `innerObject` (previously `new BatchAccountInner()`), and `isI

details.body: The constructor no longer initializes `innerObject` (previously `new BatchAccountInner()`), and `isInCreateMode()` was updated to tolerate `innerModel() == null`. However, many getters in this type call `innerModel().<prop>()` directly; if a user calls getters before `create()/refresh()` populates the inner model, this can now throw `NullPointerException`. Fix by restoring initialization of `innerObject` in the constructor (preferred), or ensure all getters safely handle a null inner model (less

## Pull request overview

Updates `azure-resourcemanager-batch` to the TypeSpec-generated (`2025-06-01`) management plane SDK, including the 3.0.0 release changes and associated model/client updates.

**Changes:**
- Regenerates Batch management client, fluent clients, and models from TypeSpec (API version `2025-06-01`).
- Introduces 3.0.0 breaking changes (notably removals around Certificates and several model types), plus adds `systemData` support across resources.
- Updates auth pipeline and u
The changes look good
@XiaofeiCao  for review

events.description: ## API Change Check  APIView identified API level changes in this PR and created the following API

details.body:
## API Change Check

APIView identified API level changes in this PR and created the following API reviews

[sdk/resourcemanager/batch/armbatch](https://spa.apiview.dev/review/5d7090769fc1444da42a31413d67c9ac?activeApiRevisionId=baa72f0c759a46bcbdc7d1d6514d7a49)
<!-- Fetch URI: https://apiview.dev/api/pullrequests?pullRequestNumber=26008&repoName=Azure/azure-sdk-for-go&commitSHA=5957551333ae9e2ede6e6deb2c20e4dc00c03291 -->

## Pull request overview

This PR migrates the Azure Batch resource manager SDK from AutoRest to TypeSpec code generation, upgrading from v3.0.1 to v4.0.0 with API version 2025-06-01. The migration removes deprecated Certificate management functionality and adds new features for disk encryption, host endpoint settings, IP family configuration, and job scheduling.

**Changes:**
- Migrated code generation from AutoRest to TypeSpec (Go Code Generator)
- Removed Certificate client and all certificat

events.description: The sample uses an invalid-looking subscription ID in `ipAddressIds` (it has an extra trailing digit

details.body: The sample uses an invalid-looking subscription ID in `ipAddressIds` (it has an extra trailing digit compared to a standard UUID format). This will cause ARM calls to fail if users copy/paste the sample. Please update the placeholder to a valid GUID-shaped value (consistent with the other samples) so the resource ID format is correct.

events.description: This sample file is named `pool_create_tags.py`, but the request body no longer includes a `tags` pr

details.body: This sample file is named `pool_create_tags.py`, but the request body no longer includes a `tags` property. Either re-add a `tags` block to the sample payload (if tags are still supported for this operation) or rename/regenerate the sample so its filename matches what it demonstrates.

## Pull request overview

This PR updates `azure-mgmt-batch` to a newly generated stable SDK for API version `2025-06-01`, including regenerated clients/models/operations and updated generated samples.

**Changes:**
- Regenerated mgmt client surface (sync + async) with updated configuration (ARM cloud endpoints, `send_request`) and updated model/enums set.
- Added new generated samples for newly supported scenarios (e.g., dual-stack networking, customer-managed keys).
- Updated packaging/metadat

events.description: Improve grammar in the sample instructions by changing 'Before run the sample' to 'Before running th

details.body: Improve grammar in the sample instructions by changing 'Before run the sample' to 'Before running the sample'.

events.description: Using `list[str]` requires Python 3.9+. If this package still supports Python 3.8 (or older), this w

details.body: Using `list[str]` requires Python 3.9+. If this package still supports Python 3.8 (or older), this will raise at import time. Please confirm the package's `python_requires` is 3.9+; otherwise, revert this annotation to `List[str]` from `typing` to preserve compatibility.

events.description: The client initialization now derives ARM endpoints from `cloud_setting` / `settings.current.azure_c

details.body: The client initialization now derives ARM endpoints from `cloud_setting` / `settings.current.azure_cloud` and relies on endpoint formatting (`{endpoint}` plus `send_request` formatting). Please add/update tests that validate request URL formatting uses the resolved ARM base URL (and does not leak the `{endpoint}` placeholder), and that `cloud_setting` overrides the default cloud as expected.
